title: leucochloron foederale
side_effects: Leucochloron foederale can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems due to its potent compounds. Ingesting the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its toxic alkaloids may result in liver and kidney damage. Additionally, improper handling of the plant may cause eye irritation, conjunctivitis, and even temporary blindness in severe cases. If not handled with caution, the side effects of Leucochloron foederale can be severe and potentially life-threatening.
